subsection.heading: In General.—
subsection.chapeau: Neither the Secretary nor the Board has jurisdiction under this part over the transportation of property by motor vehicle when—

paragraph.content: the property is transported by a person engaged in a business other than transportation; and
the transportation is within the scope of, and furthers a primary business (other than transportation) of the person.

paragraph.heading: In general.—
paragraph.content: Neither the Secretary nor the Board has jurisdiction under this part over transportation of property by motor vehicle for compensation provided by a person who is a member of a corporate family for other members of such corporate family.

paragraph.heading: Definition.—
paragraph.content: In this section, “corporate family” means a group of corporations consisting of a parent corporation and all subsidiaries in which the parent corporation owns directly or indirectly a 100 percent interest.

note.heading: Prior Provisions
note.p: Provisions similar to those in this section were contained in section 10524 of this title prior to the general amendment of this subtitle by Pub. L. 104–88, § 102(a).

note.heading: Effective Date
note.p: Section effective Jan. 1, 1996, except as otherwise provided in Pub. L. 104–88, see section 2 of Pub. L. 104–88, set out as a note under section 1301 of this title.
